_comment: Low-fidelity proof-of-concept config, history: (1) nz=40/count_max=3000/N=24 -- count_max was a guess, RuntimeError'd loudly at 13/24 (54%) non-converged, exactly as the tolerance guard is supposed to (see pipeline._init_state). (2) Ran calibrate_count_max.py at nz=40 (N=48, probe=20000) to get real data instead of guessing again -- found something worse than 'cap too low': p50=3224, p75=15325, but 9/48 (18.75%) STILL hadn't converged at 20000 steps (censored, true value unknown) -- a heavy tail nowhere near explained by count_max alone, and nz=40 had never been validated against the FULL WIDE prior before (only fixed-point smoke tests). (3) 2026-07-07, per Isaac: skip the planned nz=40-vs-50 isolation test, go straight to nz=60/art_nlayer=60 for everything -- higher than the previously-measured nz=50 baseline, to maximize the chance the heavy tail is resolved rather than iterate stepwise. count_max removed here (was the disproven 3000 guess) -- falls back to gpu_config's default (10000, also unvalidated) until calibrate_count_max.py has been rerun at THIS nz/art_nlayer and gives a real number; do not launch a non-CALIBRATE_COUNT_MAX run with this file until that's done. nu_pts=600 (R~300) still chemistry-cost-neutral (chemistry cost is band/resolution-independent) -- only shrinks opacity-table build/RT cost, unrelated to the convergence question. smc_rt_vjp_chunk=6 kept at the nu_pts=5000-probed value; art_nlayer 40->60 is a 50% RT-layer increase that hasn't been re-probed, but nu_pts=600 is so far below the validated-safe 5000 there should be ample headroom regardless. init_max_nonconverged_frac stays at config_schema.py's default (0.1).
